commit 5563e487438739023dad5c2ea4049aa55e2477ec
Author: Jiayuan Liang <ljykernel@163.com>

mm/page_owner: warn when stack trace depth hits PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH limit

This commit adds a ratelimited warning to notify developers when page_owner
silently truncates stack traces deeper than PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH.

> diff --git a/mm/page_owner.c b/mm/page_owner.c
> index 8178e0be557f..962a4f694e2d 100644
> --- a/mm/page_owner.c
> +++ b/mm/page_owner.c
> @@ -163,6 +163,9 @@ static noinline depot_stack_handle_t save_stack(gfp_t flags)
>  
>  	set_current_in_page_owner();
>  	nr_entries = stack_trace_save(entries, ARRAY_SIZE(entries), 2);
> +	if (nr_entries >= PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH)

Because stack_trace_save() bounds the returned entries to the array size,
it will return exactly PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH (16) for a perfectly fitting
stack. Does this >= condition cause false positive warnings for stacks that
fit perfectly, while also always logging the exact message "stack depth 16
exceeds limit 16"?

> +		pr_warn_ratelimited("page_owner: stack depth %u exceeds limit %u\n",
> +			nr_entries, PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH);

Since save_stack() executes during page allocations and frees, which can
occur in atomic contexts or while holding console driver locks, could an
unconditional printk() trigger an A-A self-deadlock if a console driver
triggers an allocation with a deep stack?

Additionally, does this ignore the passed flags parameter? Allocations
using __GFP_NOWARN to explicitly suppress warnings will still trigger this
printk, which violates the allocator API contract.

Is there a risk of severe performance regressions due to lock contention
here?

The pr_warn_ratelimited() macro uses a static global ratelimit state
protected by a raw spinlock. Since stack depths of 16 frames or more are
very common in the kernel, multiple CPUs frequently hitting this in the
allocator hotpath will constantly contend for the exact same global raw
spinlock.

>  	handle = stack_depot_save(entries, nr_entries, flags);
>  	if (!handle)
>  		handle = failure_handle;

    mm/page_owner: warn when stack trace depth hits PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH limit
    
    page_owner silently truncates stack traces deeper than
    PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH (16), which hides root caller information during
    memory debugging.
    
    Add a ratelimited warning to notify developers when this truncation occurs.
    
    Signed-off-by: Jiayuan Liang <ljykernel@163.com>

diff --git a/mm/page_owner.c b/mm/page_owner.c
index 8178e0be557f..962a4f694e2d 100644
--- a/mm/page_owner.c
+++ b/mm/page_owner.c
@@ -163,6 +163,9 @@ static noinline depot_stack_handle_t save_stack(gfp_t flags)
 
 	set_current_in_page_owner();
 	nr_entries = stack_trace_save(entries, ARRAY_SIZE(entries), 2);
+	if (nr_entries >= PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH)
+		pr_warn_ratelimited("page_owner: stack depth %u exceeds limit %u\n",
+			nr_entries, PAGE_OWNER_STACK_DEPTH);
 	handle = stack_depot_save(entries, nr_entries, flags);
 	if (!handle)
 		handle = failure_handle;
